segments.text: The Lord told Moses,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Tell the Israelites to turn back and set up camp near Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea. You are to camp beside the sea, opposite Baal-zephon.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Pharaoh will conclude about the Israelites: ‘They're wandering about the country in confusion—the desert has blocked them from leaving.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will give Pharaoh a stubborn attitude so that he will chase after them to get them back.  But I will gain respect through what happens to Pharaoh and all his army, and the Egyptians will know that I am the Lord.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: So the Israelites did as they were instructed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: When the king of Egypt found out that the Israelites had left in a hurry, Pharaoh and his officials changed their minds about what had happened and said,
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: What have we done? We have let all these Israelite slaves go!
segments.speaker: The Israelites did as they were

segments.text: So Pharaoh had his chariot made ready and set out with his army.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: He took 600 of his best chariots along with all the other chariots of Egypt, each with their officer-in-charge.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: The Lord gave Pharaoh, king of Egypt, a stubborn attitude so he chased after the Israelites, who were leaving with their fists raised in triumph.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: The Egyptians set out in pursuit—all Pharaoh's horses and chariots, horsemen and soldiers. They caught up with the Israelites while they were camped beside the sea near Pi-hahiroth, opposite Baal-zephon.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: The Israelites looked back and saw Pharaoh and the Egyptian army approaching. They were absolutely terrified and cried out to the Lord for help.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: They complained to Moses,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Were there no graves in Egypt that you had to bring us out here in the desert to die? What have you done to us by making us leave Egypt?
segments.speaker: The Israelites did as they were

segments.text: Didn't we tell you back in Egypt, ‘Leave us alone so that we can go on being slaves to the Egyptians’? It would have been better for us to be Egyptian slaves than to die here in the desert!
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: But Moses said to the people,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Do not be afraid. St and where you are and you will see how the Lord will save you today. The Egyptians you see right now, you will never see again!
segments.speaker: The Israelites did as they were

segments.text: The Lord is going to fight for you—you don't need to do anything.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: The Lord told Moses,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Why are you crying out to me for help? Tell the Israelites to move forward.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: You are to pick up your walking stick and hold it out in your hand over the sea. Divide it so the Israelites can walk through the sea on dry ground.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will give the Egyptians a stubborn, hard-hearted attitude so that they will chase in after them. Then I will gain respect through what happens to Pharaoh and all his army, chariots, and horsemen.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The Egyptians will know that I am the Lord when I gain respect through Pharaoh, his chariots, and his horsemen.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The angel of God, who had been leading the Israelites, moved behind them. The pillar of cloud also moved from in front of them and stood behind them,

so that it was positioned between the Egyptian and Israelite camps. The cloud was in darkness on one side, but it lit up the night on the other. No one from either camp went near the other during the night.
Then Moses held out his hand over the sea, and all through the night the Lord forced the sea back with a strong east wind, and turned the bottom of the sea into dry land. So the water was divided,
and the Israelites walked through the sea on dry ground, with walls of water to their right and left.
The Egyptians chased after them—all Pharaoh's horses, chariots, and horsemen. They followed the Israelites into the sea.
But at the end of the night the Lord looked down on the Egyptian army from the pillar of fire and cloud, and he threw them into a panic.
He made their chariot wheels get stuck so it was hard for them to drive. The Egyptians shouted out,
segments.speaker: Egyptians

segments.text: Retreat! We must run from the Israelites because the Lord is fighting for them against us!
segments.speaker: The Egyptians

segments.text: Then the Lord told Moses,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Hold out your hand over the sea, so that the water will pour back over the Egyptians and their chariots and horsemen.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So Moses held out his hand over the sea, and at dawn the sea returned to normal. As the Egyptians retreated, the Lord swept them into the sea.
The water poured back and covered the chariots and horsemen—the whole of Pharaoh's army that had chased after the Israelites into the sea. Not a single one of them survived.
But the Israelites had walked through the sea on dry ground, with walls of water to their right and left.
The Lord saved the Israelites from the threat of the Egyptians—the Israelites saw the Egyptians lying dead on the shore.
When the Israelites saw the great power that the Lord had used against the Egyptians, they were in awe of the Lord, and they trusted in him and in his servant Moses.
